topic: How to Set Up Windsurf IDE 2026: Installation, Config, and First Project

competitors.title: Setup Windsurf IDE: First Week Tips for Maximum Productivity 2026
key_points: Windsurf ships with conservative defaults — Cascade waits for explicit prompts, .windsurfrules file is blank until written
Install via Homebrew (macOS: brew install --cask windsurf) or .deb package (Ubuntu)
Create .windsurfrules file at project root to define stack, conventions, boundaries — cuts prompt length in half
Set Cascade context to Codebase mode (not default File mode) for projects under 50k lines
Use Cascade Flows (Cmd+Shift+L) for multi-file edits — describe outcomes not steps
Free tier includes 25 Cascade flow actions/month; Pro at $15/month removes the cap
Minimum 8GB RAM recommended, 16GB for Codebase context mode on larger projects
8GB RAM minimum; 16GB recommended for Codebase context mode

competitors.title: Cursor vs Windsurf (2026): Honest Comparison
key_points: Windsurf bets on autonomy — Cascade agent plans multi-step tasks, runs terminal commands, iterates on its own
Cascade completed Redis rate limiting task in 3.5 minutes vs 45-60 minutes manually (read files, wrote middleware, added env vars, applied routes, wrote tests, fixed failures)
Windsurf autocomplete is conservative — completes current line or 2-3 additional lines; Supercomplete feature suggests edits to call sites after signature changes
Cursor multi-line completions immediately usable 68-72% of the time; Windsurf is more accurate but less aggressive
Windsurf Pro at $15/month vs Cursor Pro at $20/month — Windsurf more affordable per dollar for agent-heavy users
Windsurf's Cascade can enter execution loops on complex tasks — may burn 10-15 minutes of compute on unsolvable tasks
Cascade works well about 75-80% of the time on clearly scoped tasks
Windsurf is best for prototyping, greenfield projects, and developers who prefer describing outcomes

competitors.title: Windsurf AI IDE Statistics 2026: Users, Revenue & Adoption
key_points: Windsurf has 1M+ active users as of March 2026 (doubled from 500k+ in 2024)
70M+ lines of AI-written code per day; 94% of code output is AI-generated
4,000+ enterprises in production; 59% of Fortune 500 companies build with Windsurf
Reported $82M ARR (Reuters, July 2025); earlier estimates ranged $40M-$100M in early 2025
ServiceNow deployed Windsurf across ~7,000 engineers with 10% productivity boost (The New Stack)
Enterprise claims: 50-200% developer velocity improvement, 20-40x ROI, 60-80% code automation by Cascade
Pricing: Free ($0), Pro ($15/month), Teams ($30/user/month), custom enterprise pricing
SOC 2 Type II, FedRAMP High, HIPAA compliance; enterprise features include RBAC, SSO, hybrid deployment
Acquired by Cognition AI in 2025 after reported $3B OpenAI deal and Google's $2.4B talent-and-licensing deal

competitors.title: Welcome to Windsurf — Official Documentation
key_points: Download available for Mac (minimum OS X Yosemite), Windows, Ubuntu, and other Linux distributions
Onboarding flow: select setup (Start fresh, Import from VS Code, Import from Cursor), choose theme, sign up/log in
Supports VS Code keybindings or Vim bindings during setup
Key features to try: Write with Cascade, Generate a project, Open Folder/Remote Server, Configure Settings, Command Palette
Windsurf Next is a prerelease version with newest features (opt-in)
Can import VS Code/Cursor configurations after onboarding via Command Palette
Some extensions are incompatible (other AI code complete extensions, proprietary extensions)
Uninstall: close app, delete application, remove ~/.codeium/windsurf config folder, clean PATH

competitors.title: VS Code vs Cursor vs Windsurf: Which AI-Powered IDE Should You Use in 2026?
key_points: Windsurf cold start time: 3.4 seconds; AI completion latency: 102ms; 'Explain This Function': 2.8s
Windsurf idle memory: ~1.1GB; no native Dev Container support
Windsurf strengths: real-time collaboration, polished UI, AI-native architecture
Windsurf weaknesses: resource overhead, no Dev Container support
Free plan available with usage caps; Pro plan at $15/month
Windsurf uses Codeium's proprietary models alongside third-party models for Cascade and chat
